mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals aggregate income expansion and forward earnings power. Persistent payroll gains reduce recession probability, lower equity risk premia, and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138495 thousand persons as of t-1mo
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS changes do not significantly exceed returns following negative changes (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt-service burdens, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) captures a broad-based recovery in manufacturing output and construction activity. The interaction effect strengthens the growth signal beyond either series in isolation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.9019 and HOUST at 1187.0 as of t-2mo
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and HOUST does not significantly predict 60-day S&P 500 returns (regression p > 0.05) or the directional hit rate falls below 55% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5.5%, the economy is operating near full employment and further declines signal wage pressures and impending monetary tightening. At the current UNRATE of 5.0%, a marginal decline indicates late-cycle overheating risk that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 5.5% and declining over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signal nominal demand pressure and revenue growth for listed corporations. In the short term, nominal earnings reprice faster than input costs, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and 5-day S&P 500 forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) captures durable aggregate demand expansion and capacity absorption. Stronger GDP growth supports upward earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11658.9 billion chained 2017 dollars as of t-6mo
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1% signals a sudden negative demand shock that triggers de-risking by equity investors. The consumption shortfall translates into immediate revenue misses and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 382928 million dollars as of t-1mo
failure_condition: RSAFS declines exceeding 1% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects rising capacity utilization and operating leverage in the corporate sector. Higher output momentum supports margin expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns with a hit rate above 55% over a 3-year rolling window.
